text:
Once upon a time, in a bustling city filled with skyscrapers and whispers, there lived a fox named Freddy. Freddy loved exploring the city at night. He’d climb up on skyscraper antennaes, feel the wind on his fur, and gaze at the starry sky.

One night, Freddy stumbled upon an old book shop tucked away behind a maze of buildings. The signs were faded, but an inviting smell wafted from inside. Inside, the shopkeeper smiled warmly.

“Aren’t you just quaint, Freddy?” she said. “Find anything you like?”

“You know,” Freddy replied, flipping through pages. “I was curious about dreams.”

The shopkeeper handed him an ancient tome. Its cover smelled like earthy moss. Inside, dreams were detailed tales.

Freddy spent weeks reading it. One night, under the moonlight, he dreamt he’d landed on a farm. Chickens talked, and the sky was teal. He felt such peace there.
